description_long: This object consists of a rectangular section of cobblestone pavement embedded with six large, round manhole covers of varying diameters. The manhole covers are made of weathered metal, each featuring a central square or circular indentation, and are arranged in a somewhat irregular pattern. The cobblestones are tightly packed and bordered by a row of reddish-brown bricks, forming a clear boundary around the section. The metal covers show signs of age, with surface discoloration and some moss or dirt in the crevices. The overall surface is mostly flat, with the manhole covers slightly raised above the cobblestones. This setup is typical for urban environments where multiple access points to underground utilities are required.
description: A rectangular cobblestone pavement section with six weathered metal manhole covers of different sizes, bordered by reddish bricks. The covers are arranged irregularly and show signs of age and use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arrangement of all six manhole covers, with the largest cover near the center and the brick border clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the flatness of the surface and the relative heights of the manhole covers above the cobblestones.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the irregular spacing and size differences among the manhole covers, with the brick border receding.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the covers from a different angle, with the cobblestone and brick textures prominent.
attribute_pattern_design: The manhole covers have plain surfaces with central square or circular indentations, and the cobblestone pavement features a regular grid of small, rectangular stones bordered by bricks.
